weather.summary: Saturday: 16°C, broken clouds. Sunday: 16°C, scattered clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend is shaping up to be fantastic for outdoor adventures! With mild temperatures and mostly clear skies, it's perfect for exploring London's parks and gardens. I've found some delightful activities, including a Holi Colour Dance Festival and free museum events, perfect for a 3-year-old to enjoy.

activities.title: Tiny Planet
activities.description: A heartwarming show, but appropriate for slightly older children.
activities.whyItWorks: While the age range is slightly older, some 3-year-olds may still enjoy the visuals and simple storytelling.

transport.tip: Check showtimes for best travel options.

activities.title: V&A Museum of Childhood
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: A museum designed specifically for children, with interactive exhibits focused on design and creativity.
activities.whyItWorks: It's free, and hands-on exhibits encourage play and learning. There are sensory playscapes and an Imagination Playground.

transport.tip: Avoid peak hours for a less crowded experience.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A city farm with a variety of animals and a community feel.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals are always a hit with toddlers. It's free and near Brick Lane.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Shoreditch High Street
transport.tip: Check for special Easter activities closer to the date.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A small, beautifully designed wooden playground with a pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: It's designed for toddlers, and the pond theme is engaging.

transport.tip: Combine with a visit to the Olympic Park.

activities.title: Mudchute Park and Farm
activities.address: Pier Street, Isle of Dogs, London E14 3HP
activities.description: One of the largest city farms, with free entry and many animals.
activities.whyItWorks: It's free and has plenty of animals for toddlers to see.

transport.tip: Check for special Easter activities.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm with animals and gardening activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Local and easy to access. Great for a quick visit with animals.

transport.tip: Check for seasonal workshops.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Vast parklands with playgrounds and open spaces to explore.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space to run around and explore. Great for outdoor play.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses and trains to Stratford
transport.tip: Check the park's website for events.

activities.title: Last Unicorn Airways
activities.address: Lyric Square, King St, London W6 0QL
activities.description: Playful storytelling that encourages seeing everyday life as an adventure.
activities.whyItWorks: While aimed at slightly older children, the immersive aspects may engage a 3-year-old.

transport.tip: Check showtimes and book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.address: Cromwell Rd, South Kensington, London SW7 5BD
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, fascinating creatures, and intriguing science.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs are always a hit. Free and lots to see.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Rd, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Wonderlab offers hands-on experiences. Free entry.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Book tickets for Wonderlab in advance.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.address: 100 London Rd, Forest Hill, London SE23 3PQ
activities.description: Explore natural history, musical instruments, and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Animal Walk, gardens, and family craft activities.

transport.tip: Check for family events on Saturdays.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo in Hampstead Heath with rare birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: It's free, and toddlers love animals.

transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated to children, with an adventure playground, sand pits, and a city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: It's free, safe, and designed for children.

transport.tip: Adults only allowed if accompanying a child.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A playground with a Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship and sensory trails.
activities.whyItWorks: The pirate ship is a major attraction.

transport.tip: Can get crowded, especially on weekends.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.address: King William Walk, Greenwich, London SE10 9HT
activities.description: Participate in an Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship. Discover fascinating facts and unscramble a secret nautical code for a chocolatey treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun activity, discovering new facts

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: A large park with gardens, playgrounds, and a mini zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for picnics, relaxing walks, and has a playground.

transport.tip: check out the Pump House Gallery

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for giant Easter eggs hidden around the park. Crack a secret password.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animals with an Easter egg hunt.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam the gardens to find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues and win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Easter egg hunt in a historical setting.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Liverpool Street to Hampton Court
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Fairy Tales at the British Library
activities.description: An interactive exhibition exploring enchanted lands, magical creatures, and timeless tales.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and tactile exhibits.

transport.tip: Go early to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: A fun-filled family Easter adventure with installations to find bees and help them build a hive.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and engaging for the target age.

transport.tip: Book garden tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.address: 101 Queen Caroline Street, Hammersmith, London, W6 9BN
activities.description: A theatrical performance of the classic story.
activities.whyItWorks: Classic story to captivate a child's imagination.

transport.tip: Check showtimes for best travel options.

activities.title: Peppa Pig's egg-stra special Easter Afternoon Tea Bus Tour
activities.description: A Peppa Pig themed afternoon tea on a London bus with an Easter egg hunt activity.
activities.whyItWorks: If your child loves Peppa Pig, this is a treat.

transport.tip: Check departure location when booking.

activities.title: Paddington Bear Easter Trail
activities.description: A city-wide Easter treasure trail featuring Paddington Bear.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun challenges and interactive storytelling with a beloved character.

transport.tip: Check locations and transport routes before you go.

activities.title: Royal Institution's Family Fun Day: Spectacular Space
activities.description: Volunteers, scientists and engineers run a range of age-appropriate activities on topics including galaxies, moons, telescopes and space toilets.
activities.whyItWorks: This family fun day is amazing.

transport.tip: Check locations and transport routes before you go.
